entries.title: Double Agents: Exposing Security Blind Spots in GCP Vertex AI
entries.summary: Unit 42 uncovers a "double agent" flaw in Google Cloud's Vertex AI, demonstrating how overprivileged AI agents can compromise cloud environments. The post Double Agents: Exposing Security Blind Spots in GCP Vertex AI appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Threat Brief: March 2026 Escalation of Cyber Risk Related to Iran (Updated March 26)
entries.summary: Unit 42 details recent Iranian cyberattack activity, sharing direct observations of phishing, hacktivist activity and cybercrime. We include recommendations for defenders. The post Threat Brief: March 2026 Escalation of Cyber Risk Related to Iran (Updated March 26) appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Converging Interests: Analysis of Threat Clusters Targeting a Southeast Asian Government
entries.summary: Unit 42 uncovers multiple clusters of cyberespionage targeting a Southeast Asian government organization with USBFect, RATs and loaders. The post Converging Interests: Analysis of Threat Clusters Targeting a Southeast Asian Government appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Threat Brief: Recruiting Scheme Impersonating Palo Alto Networks Talent Acquisition Team
entries.summary: Unit 42 identifies a recruitment phishing campaign targeting senior professionals via impersonation and fraudulent resume fees. The post Threat Brief: Recruiting Scheme Impersonating Palo Alto Networks Talent Acquisition Team appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Google Cloud Authenticator: The Hidden Mechanisms of Passwordless Authentication
entries.summary: Explore Google’s synced passkey architecture. Unit 42 details its mechanisms, key management, and secure communication in passwordless systems." The post Google Cloud Authenticator: The Hidden Mechanisms of Passwordless Authentication appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Who’s Really Shopping? Retail Fraud in the Age of Agentic AI

entries.title: Analyzing the Current State of AI Use in Malware
entries.summary: Unit 42 research explores how AI is currently used in malware, from superficial integrations to advanced decision-making, and its future impact. The post Analyzing the Current State of AI Use in Malware appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Navigating Security Tradeoffs of AI Agents
entries.summary: Unit 42 outlines the risks of AI ecosystems and allowing AI agents excessive privileges. Learn how to keep your security strategy up to date with these latest trends. The post Navigating Security Tradeoffs of AI Agents appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Open, Closed and Broken: Prompt Fuzzing Finds LLMs Still Fragile Across Open and Closed Models

entries.title: Boggy Serpens Threat Assessment
entries.summary: Iranian threat group Boggy Serpens' cyberespionage evolves with AI-enhanced malware and refined social engineering. Unit 42 details their persistent targeting. The post Boggy Serpens Threat Assessment appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Iranian Cyber Threat Evolution: From MBR Wipers to Identity Weaponization
entries.summary: The evolution of Iranian cyber operations in broad context: from custom wiper malware to misuse of legitimate admin tools and more. The post Iranian Cyber Threat Evolution: From MBR Wipers to Identity Weaponization appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Insights: Increased Risk of Wiper Attacks
entries.summary: We are observing an increase of wiper attacks by the Iran-linked Handala Hack group (aka Void Manticore) through phishing and misuse of Microsoft Intune. The post Insights: Increased Risk of Wiper Attacks appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.summary: An espionage operation demonstrated strategic operational patience against targets in Southeast Asia, deploying custom backdoors. The post Suspected China-Based Espionage Operation Against Military Targets in Southeast Asia appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Controls
entries.summary: Unit 42 research reveals AI judges are vulnerable to stealthy prompt injection. Benign formatting symbols can bypass security controls. The post Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Controls appeared first on Unit 42 .

entries.title: An Investigation Into Years of Undetected Operations Targeting High-Value Sectors
entries.summary: In-depth analysis of threat activity we call CL-UNK-1068. We discuss their toolset, including tunneling, reconnaissance and credential theft. The post An Investigation Into Years of Undetected Operations Targeting High-Value Sectors appeared first on Unit 42 .
